WattleBlock Group Ltd — Internal Incident Report (Confidential; not for market release).
Prepared by Ms. Carla Wynn, Chief Information Security Officer, 2 June 2026.
On 1 June 2026 we detected unauthorised access via a phishing-induced SSO token reuse at our vendor Kangaroo Ridge Cloud Pty Ltd affecting the shared drive WB-ESG-Deals and a staging SFTP endpoint.
Exposed materials include a draft term sheet for the RainTree Carbon Token pilot and custody MOU with Tinara Custody Pty Ltd, internal ESG emissions datasets, and an employee CSV containing names, work emails, and vaccination status.
The term sheet contains non-public commercial terms (AUD 4.8m seed allocation, 12% warrant coverage, fee rebate triggers tied to token float) and is considered potentially price sensitive under the Corporations Act and ASX Listing Rule 3.1.
We have imposed an immediate trading blackout under our Securities Trading Policy and updated the insider list WB-IL-2026-06-01 to include all staff in Tokenisation and Sustainability Finance.
An information barrier is now in place ring‑fencing the RainTree listing team from Sales and Research, and no external briefings are permitted to avoid tipping or selective disclosure.
Personal information likely affected includes Mr. Jai Moran (Product Analyst, jai.moran@wattleblock.com.au) and Ms. Fiona Beale (Compliance Associate, f.beale@wattleblock.com.au); two candidate background check PDFs from HR also include credit screening summaries, which remain unaltered.
Operational review shows no movement from production cold wallets and no customer balances affected; the training file that lists demo wallet 0xA1B2C3D4 and “insider list” examples is educational and uses placeholders only.
Selective disclosure risk was identified after a draft investor deck was emailed by a contractor to Granite River Capital Pty Ltd before cleansing; a recall was issued and we have obtained written confirmation of deletion.
The ESG dataset includes FY25–FY27 Scope 1–3 assumptions supporting our planned sustainability‑linked note coupon ratchet and upcoming carbon procurement volumes, which have not been announced to the market.
We are assessing obligations under the Privacy Act Notifiable Data Breach scheme and will notify the privacy regulator and affected individuals within statutory timeframes if serious harm is likely.
A draft holding announcement is being prepared for release pending materiality assessment, and the blackout will remain in place until approval and lodgement via the market announcements platform.
Contact: Ms. Carla Wynn, ciso@wattleblock.com.au, +61 2 5557 9800; Legal liaison: Dr. Owen Park, o.park@wattleblock.com.au; all media queries to communications@wattleblock.com.au only.
